sanskrit: यथा प्रदीप्तं ज्वलनं पतङ्गा विशन्ति नाशाय समृद्धवेगाः। तथैव नाशाय विशन्ति लोकास्तवापि वक्त्राणि समृद्धवेगाः॥
transliteration: yathā pradīptaṃ jvalanaṃ pataṅgā viśanti nāśāya samṛddha-vegāḥ | tathaiva nāśāya viśanti lokās tavāpi vaktrāṇi samṛddha-vegāḥ ||
literal_meaning: As moths enter a blazing fire with full speed for destruction, so too all beings enter Your mouths with full speed for destruction.
contextual_meaning: Arjuna perceives the cosmic devourer: every human impulse—his own and others’—rushing toward annihilation within the Absolute. The ego’s instinctive drive to survive is seen as a moth-like compulsion that ends in self-extinction, revealing the unconscious death-wish beneath worldly striving.
psychological_state: Acute mortality-awareness with depersonalization; the self is both spectator and participant in a collective rush toward symbolic death.
trigger_scenario: Scrolling a live feed of global crises—climate refugees, pandemic footage, war drones—feeling each pixel is a life, including one’s own, being swallowed by an algorithmic maw that no protest can stop.
anchor_text: Watch urges draw you like moths; choose conscious action over blind rush.
